title: Richly Spiced Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring lamb, rum, and a blend of spices, cooked in a tagine and finished with a flambe, this recipe is shojin-friendly and perfect for a special occasion, the combination of grassy and shattering flavors with the monsoon moroccan spices creates a unique taste experience.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until it is lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, turmeric, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the rum and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Use a match or lighter to flambe the rum, being careful not to splash any of the liquid.
Simmer the tagine for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
